name: Braga Transportation Cozy Bus
districts_or_customers: Fall River Public Schools (listed as a school transportation provider for the city)
recent_news: BBB lists business start as 6/10/1986 (~39 years operating); USDOT (#4034710) currently inactive, suggesting possible scale-back or family-only operation
website: https://www.cozyautotours.com (Cozy Bus Company / Braga Transportation Inc.)
fit_notes: Small family-run Fall River MA operator (~20 employees, ~$1M revenue, 39-yr history). Aging founder Bob Braga and inactive USDOT suggest meaningful succession opportunity. Sub-scale for a stand-alone tuck-in but could fold into a larger MA Summit footprint if local district contracts are intact.

name: JFK Transportation
districts_or_customers: Southern California schools (LA to San Diego corridor); operates from Santa Ana, San Gabriel, and Glendale locations
recent_news: Self-described family-owned since 1993; expanded to 3 SoCal offices; no recent M&A or growth announcements found
fit_notes: 32-yr family-owned SoCal charter/school transport with 3 offices in Santa Ana, San Gabriel, Glendale. Small (~$0.75M revenue) but covers attractive LA/OC market. Likely too small/charter-skewed for a primary platform tuck-in but possible bolt-on in a CA roll-up.

name: Duram School Svc L P
owner_or_parent: Durham School Services LP - a subsidiary of National Express Group plc (UK); now part of the recently rebranded 'Summit School Services' platform per Wikipedia redirect
principals: Tom Buntt (local Manager per source data)
Durham School Services LP corporate leadership (National Express LLC, Lisle IL HQ)
fleet_size_est: Local Auburn yard est. small (~15-25 buses for Placer County area); parent fleet ~12,000 buses nationwide
districts_or_customers: Placer County / Auburn area school districts; parent serves 250+ districts in 25+ states
recent_news: Wikipedia entry shows 'Durham School Services' redirects to 'Summit School Services,' indicating the brand consolidation under I Squared Capital's platform is already underway
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target - this is the local Auburn CA yard of Durham School Services LP, which is the Summit School Services platform itself (National Express US operations owned by I Squared). Already in-house; flag for record cleanup in the prospect list.

name: Mercy Transportation
principals: Barbara Wood (Manager, per source data) - unverified
fleet_size_est: unknown - business appears defunct at this address
districts_or_customers: Possibly Broward County area special-needs/disability transport (historical)
recent_news: Yellow Pages now lists the Plantation FL address (1876 N University Dr, Ste 201) as CLOSED; FMCSA SAFER shows no active 'Mercy Transportation' carrier in Plantation; mercytrans.com is a separate Monmouth Junction NJ operator (founded 2003) and not the same company
website: None active for this entity (mercytrans.com belongs to a different NJ operator)
fit_notes: Plantation FL Mercy Transportation appears to be CLOSED per Yellow Pages and no active FMCSA registration found at this address. Likely a dead lead - recommend removing from active pipeline. The data-source website 'mercytrans.com' is a separate NJ company and should not be confused with this entity.

name: Medride Corp
owner_or_parent: independent (MedRide Corporation / MedRide/Oklahoma Critical Care Transport)
principals: David Batson - CEO/Owner (per D&B and source data)
Bobby (operations contact per website email bobby@medride.com)
fleet_size_est: ~40-60 wheelchair vans/ambulances (est. from 80 employees, 24/7 dispatch, two metro coverage)
districts_or_customers: Oklahoma City Metro and Tulsa Metro (statewide OK); medical clinics, dialysis centers, hospitals; member of The Transportation Alliance
recent_news: No M&A news found; operates a sister brand 'Oklahoma Critical Care Transport' (MedRide/OCCT) suggesting expansion into higher-acuity medical transport
fit_notes: Pure-play NEMT/critical care operator - NOT a school-bus or SPED contractor. ~$5M revenue, 80 employees, 24-yr operating history under founder David Batson. Outside Summit's core school-bus thesis; would be relevant only if Summit pursues an NEMT/SPED-adjacent vertical. Recommend de-prioritize unless special-needs school transport is verified.
